jobs.title: P2609 - Senior Trial Master File (TMF) Specialist
jobs.description:
Debiopharm is an independent biopharmaceutical company based in Switzerland with an ongoing commitment to research in oncology and infectious diseases in order to improve patient outcomes and quality of life. Our main activities include drug development, drug manufacturing and digital health investment. Debiopharm International SA aims to develop innovative therapies that target high unmet medical needs. Our unique “development only” business model allows us to act as a bridge between disruptive discovery products and patient access in international markets. We first identify high-potential compounds for in-licensing, clinically demonstrate their safety and efficacy and then select large pharmaceutical commercialization partners to maximize patient access globally.

At Debiopharm you will be at the forefront of the fight against cancer and infectious diseases, and you will have the opportunity to work on new innovative medicines for patients. Efficiency is our mode of action, saving lives our collective target.

We are currently looking for a motivated and collaborative professional to join our Global Clinical Operations Department (R&D) based at our Headquarters in Lausanne, Switzerland as

Senior Trial Master File (TMF) Specialist

Permanent role | Lausanne, Switzerland

Your Mission: Ensuring Excellence and Inspection-Readiness of Clinical Trial Documentation

As a Senior TMF Specialist, you will be the guardian of our Trial Master Files, supporting Clinical Trial Teams from trial setup through to final archiving. Your role is critical in ensuring that our clinical documentation is compliant, of the highest quality, and always ready for regulatory inspection.

Your Key Responsibilities

## Requirements
- Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Science, Healthcare, or equivalent experience in a relevant discipline.
- Experience: At least 3 years in the pharmaceutical industry, with hands-on experience in clinical trials and TMF documentation/systems.
- Expertise: Solid knowledge of Good Clinical Practice (GCP) and advanced proficiency in managing digital clinical systems.
- Languages: Fluent in English; proficiency in French or another European language is a distinct asset.
- Soft Skills: A strong communicator and team leader capable of building effective relationships across internal and external functions. You are highly organized, detail-oriented, and excel at planning.

## Benefits
- Being part of a company where innovation, collaboration, and impact aren’t just values — they’re how we work every day
- Partner with teams across disciplines, at the forefront of oncology and anti-infective development
- An inclusive and respectful workplace — proud to be Equal-Pay certified
- Grow in a culture that values people, purpose, and performance
- A chance to grow, share, and shape the future of healthcare

Innanzitutto identifichiamo composti ad alto potenziale per il rilascio in licenza, dimostriamo clinicamente la loro sicurezza ed efficacia e quindi selezioniamo grandi partner di commercializzazione farmaceutica per massimizzare l'accesso dei pazienti a livello globale. In Debiopharm sarai in prima linea nella lotta contro il cancro e le malattie infettive e avrai l'opportunità di lavorare su nuovi farmaci innovativi per i pazienti. descriptionByLocale.fr:
Debiopharm est une société biopharmaceutique indépendante basée en Suisse qui s'engage en permanence dans la recherche en oncologie et en maladies infectieuses afin d'améliorer les résultats pour les patients et la qualité de vie. Nos principales activités comprennent le développement de médicaments, la fabrication de médicaments et l'investissement dans la santé numérique. Debiopharm International SA vise à développer des thérapies innovantes qui ciblent des besoins médicaux non satisfaits élevés. Notre modèle commercial unique de « développement uniquement » nous permet d'agir comme un pont entre les produits de découverte perturbateurs et l'accès des patients aux marchés internationaux. Nous identifions d'abord les composés à fort potentiel pour l'octroi de licences, démontrons cliniquement leur innocuité et leur efficacité, puis sélectionnons de grands partenaires de commercialisation pharmaceutique afin de maximiser l'accès des patients à l'échelle mondiale. Chez Debiopharm, vous serez à l'avant-garde de la lutte contre le cancer et les maladies infectieuses, et vous aurez l'occasion de travailler sur de nouveaux médicaments innovants pour les patients. L'efficacité est notre mode d'action, sauver des vies est notre objectif collectif. Nous sommes actuellement à la recherche d'un professionnel motivé et collaboratif pour rejoindre notre département des opérations cliniques mondiales (R&D) basé à notre siège à Lausanne, en Suisse, en tant que spécialiste principal du dossier principal des essais (TMF) Rôle permanent | Lausanne, Suisse 
• Votre mission : assurer l'excellence et l'inspection - Préparation de la documentation des essais cliniques En tant que spécialiste principal du TMF, vous serez le gardien de nos dossiers principaux des essais, soutenant les équipes d'essais cliniques

jobs.title: P2610 - Administrative Assistant - Part-time
jobs.description:
Debiopharm is an independent biopharmaceutical company based in Switzerland with an ongoing commitment to research in oncology and infectious diseases in order to improve patient outcomes and quality of life. Our main activities include drug development, drug manufacturing and digital health investment. Debiopharm International SA aims to develop innovative therapies that target high unmet medical needs. Our unique “development only” business model allows us to act as a bridge between disruptive discovery products and patient access in international markets. We first identify high-potential compounds for in-licensing, clinically demonstrate their safety and efficacy and then select large pharmaceutical commercialization partners to maximize patient access globally.

At Debiopharm you will be at the forefront of the fight against cancer and infectious diseases, and you will have the opportunity to work on new innovative medicines for patients. Efficiency is our mode of action, saving lives our collective target.

We are looking for a proactive and highly organized professional to support the Head of Commercial Alliance Management Team, based at our Headquarters in Lausanne, Switzerland, as

Administrative Assistant – 50%

Contractor Permanent position | Lausanne, Switzerland

Your Mission: Serve as the operational pillar for the Head of BD and Global Commercial Alliance Management and team.

You will ensure total fluidity in their professional daily life, with a specific focus on complex agenda management, congress logistics, and the organization of high-stakes client meetings.

We require availability for a minimum of 4 hours every working day, with flexibility to structure those hours in the mornings or afternoons.

Your Responsibilities

## Requirements
- Education & languages: Bachelor’s degree, CFC, or equivalent, with excellent written and verbal communication skills in both English and French.
- Experience: Minimum of 5 years of relevant and transferable experience, in an international and fast-paced environment
- Technical expertise: Tech-savvy in Microsoft Office 365 applications (Teams, SharePoint, PowerPoint).
- Soft skills:
- Absolute discretion, professionalism, and a high level of confidentiality.
- Ability and agility to anticipate roadblocks, manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
- Team player with strong interpersonal skills and a service-oriented mindset.
- Proactive stakeholders’ engagement and management, with transparent communication.

## Benefits
- Join a company at the forefront of innovation in oncology and anti-infective development
- Work in an inclusive and respectful environment, proud to be Equal-Pay certified
- Grow within a culture that values people, purpose, and performance

Debiopharm ist ein unabhängiges biopharmazeutisches Unternehmen mit Sitz in der Schweiz, das sich kontinuierlich der Forschung in den Bereichen Onkologie und Infektionskrankheiten widmet, um die Behandlungsergebnisse und die Lebensqualität der Patienten zu verbessern. Zu unseren Hauptaktivitäten gehören Arzneimittelentwicklung, Arzneimittelherstellung und Investitionen in die digitale Gesundheit. Ziel von Debiopharm International SA ist die Entwicklung innovativer Therapien, die auf einen hohen ungedeckten medizinischen Bedarf abzielen. Unser einzigartiges Geschäftsmodell „nur Entwicklung“ ermöglicht es uns, als Brücke zwischen bahnbrechenden Entdeckungsprodukten und Patientenzugang auf internationalen Märkten zu fungieren. Wir identifizieren zunächst Wirkstoffe mit hohem Potenzial für die Einlizenzierung, demonstrieren klinisch deren Sicherheit und Wirksamkeit und wählen dann große Pharmavermarktungspartner aus, um den Patientenzugang weltweit zu maximieren. Bei Debiopharm stehen Sie an vorderster Front im Kampf gegen Krebs und Infektionskrankheiten und haben die Möglichkeit, an neuen innovativen Medikamenten für Patienten zu arbeiten. Effizienz ist unsere Vorgehensweise, Leben retten unser gemeinsames Ziel. Wir suchen einen proaktiven und gut organisierten Fachmann zur Unterstützung des Leiters des Commercial Alliance Management Teams an unserem Hauptsitz in Lausanne, Schweiz, als Verwaltungsassistent – ​​50 % Auftragnehmer, Festanstellung | Lausanne, Schweiz Ihre Mission: Dienen Sie als operative Säule für den Head of BD und das Global Commercial Alliance Management und Team. Sie sorgen für einen reibungslosen Ablauf in ihrem beruflichen Alltag, mit besonderem Fokus auf komplexes Agenda-Management, Kongresslogistik und die Organisation hochkarätiger Kundenmeetings. Wir benötigen eine Verfügbarkeit von mindestens 4 Stunden an jedem Arbeitstag, mit der Flexibilität, diese Stunden morgens oder nachmittags zu strukturieren. 
• Ihre Aufgaben ## 
• Anforderungen – Ausbildung und Sprachen: Bachelor-Abschluss, CFC oder gleichwertig, mit hervorragenden schriftlichen und mündlichen Kommunikationsfähigkeiten in Englisch und Französisch. - Erfahrung: Mindestens 5 Jahre relevante und übertragbare Erfahrung in einem internationalen und schnelllebigen Umfeld. - Technisches Fachwissen: Technisch versiert in Microsoft Office 365-Anwendungen (Teams, SharePoint, PowerPoint). - Soft Skills: - Absolute Diskretion, Professionalität und ein hohes Maß an Vertraulichkeit. - Fähigkeit und Agilität, Hindernisse zu antizipieren und mehrere Prioritäten in einer schnelllebigen Umgebung zu verwalten. - Teamplayer mit starken zwischenmenschlichen Fähigkeiten und einer serviceorientierten Denkweise. - Proaktive Einbindung und Verwaltung der Stakeholder mit transparenter Kommunikation. ## Vorteile – Werden Sie Teil eines Unternehmens, das an der Spitze der Innovation in der Onkologie und der Entwicklung von Antiinfektiva steht – Arbeiten Sie in einem integrativen und respektvollen Umfeld und sind stolz auf die Equal-Pay-Zertifizierung – Wachsen Sie in einer Kultur, die Menschen, Ziele und Leistung wertschätzt

jobs.title: P2611 - Senior Legal Counsel - Commercial & Privacy
jobs.description:
Debiopharm is an independent biopharmaceutical company based in Switzerland with an ongoing commitment to research in oncology and infectious diseases in order to improve patient outcomes and quality of life. Our main activities include drug development, drug manufacturing and digital health investment. Debiopharm International SA aims to develop innovative therapies that target high unmet medical needs. Our unique “development only” business model allows us to act as a bridge between disruptive discovery products and patient access in international markets. We first identify high-potential compounds for in-licensing, clinically demonstrate their safety and efficacy and then select large pharmaceutical commercialization partners to maximize patient access globally.

At Debiopharm you will be at the forefront of the fight against cancer and infectious diseases, and you will have the opportunity to work on new innovative medicines for patients. Efficiency is our mode of action, saving lives our collective target.

We are currently looking for a motivated and collaborative professional to join our Legal Department based at our Headquarters in Lausanne, Switzerland as

Senior Legal Counsel – Commercial & Privacy

Permanent role | Lausanne, Switzerland

Your Mission: Protecting Innovation through Legal Excellence

As a Legal Counsel – Commercial & Privacy, you will serve as a pragmatic, business-oriented partner to our commercial, clinical, and operational teams. You will safeguard and develop Debiopharm’s interests by managing a diverse portfolio of complex contracts and driving our global data protection compliance programme.

Your Key 
• Responsibilities

· Commercial & Strategic Contracting: Draft and negotiate a broad range of life sciences agreements, including MSAs, Licenses, Asset Purchases, Manufacturing, and Clinical Trial Agreements (CTAs).

· Data Privacy Leadership: Oversee compliance with Swiss FADP and EU GDPR, providing expert guidance on clinical trial privacy, patient data, and cross-border transfers.

· Risk Mitigation: Identify and anticipate legal risks in business activities, offering actionable solutions to facilitate objectives while protecting company rights.

· Healthcare Compliance: Apply strong knowledge of industry-specific frameworks, including anti-bribery/anti-corruption laws and interactions with Healthcare Professionals.

· Internal Training & Evolution: Enhance legal awareness across the organization by training employees and updating internal policies and templates.

· Dispute Management: Manage pre-litigation strategies and resolve disputes pragmatically, directing external counsel when necessary.

You will safeguard and develop Debiopharm’s interests by managing a diverse portfolio of complex contracts and driving our global data protection compliance programme.

Your Key 
• Responsibilities

· Commercial & Strategic Contracting: Draft and negotiate a broad range of life sciences agreements, including MSAs, Licenses, Asset Purchases, Manufacturing, and Clinical Trial Agreements (CTAs).

· Data Privacy Leadership: Oversee compliance with Swiss FADP and EU GDPR, providing expert guidance on clinical trial privacy, patient data, and cross-border transfers.

· Risk Mitigation: Identify and anticipate legal risks in business activities, offering actionable solutions to facilitate objectives while protecting company rights.

· Healthcare Compliance: Apply strong knowledge of industry-specific frameworks, including anti-bribery/anti-corruption laws and interactions with Healthcare Professionals.

· Internal Training & Evolution: Enhance legal awareness across the organization by training employees and updating internal policies and templates.

· Dispute Management: Manage pre-litigation strategies and resolve disputes pragmatically, directing external counsel when necessary.

descriptionByLocale.de:
Debiopharm ist ein unabhängiges biopharmazeutisches Unternehmen mit Sitz in der Schweiz, das sich kontinuierlich der Forschung in den Bereichen Onkologie und Infektionskrankheiten widmet, um die Behandlungsergebnisse und die Lebensqualität der Patienten zu verbessern. Zu unseren Hauptaktivitäten gehören Arzneimittelentwicklung, Arzneimittelherstellung und Investitionen in die digitale Gesundheit. Ziel von Debiopharm International SA ist die Entwicklung innovativer Therapien, die auf einen hohen ungedeckten medizinischen Bedarf abzielen. Unser einzigartiges Geschäftsmodell „nur Entwicklung“ ermöglicht es uns, als Brücke zwischen bahnbrechenden Entdeckungsprodukten und Patientenzugang auf internationalen Märkten zu fungieren. Wir identifizieren zunächst Wirkstoffe mit hohem Potenzial für die Einlizenzierung, demonstrieren klinisch deren Sicherheit und Wirksamkeit und wählen dann große Pharmavermarktungspartner aus, um den Patientenzugang weltweit zu maximieren. Bei Debiopharm stehen Sie an vorderster Front im Kampf gegen Krebs und Infektionskrankheiten und haben die Möglichkeit, an neuen innovativen Medikamenten für Patienten zu arbeiten. Effizienz ist unsere Vorgehensweise, Leben retten unser gemeinsames Ziel. Wir sind derzeit auf der Suche nach einer motivierten und kooperativen Fachkraft für die Verstärkung unserer Rechtsabteilung an unserem Hauptsitz in Lausanne, Schweiz, als Senior Legal Counsel – Commercial & Privacy, Festanstellung | Lausanne, Schweiz Ihre Mission: Schutz von Innovationen durch juristische Exzellenz. Als Rechtsberater – Kommerziell und Datenschutz fungieren Sie als pragmatischer, geschäftsorientierter Partner für unsere kommerziellen, klinischen und operativen Teams. Sie schützen und entwickeln die Interessen von Debiopharm, indem Sie ein vielfältiges Portfolio komplexer Verträge verwalten und unser globales Datenschutz-Compliance-Programm vorantreiben. 
• Ihre Hauptaufgaben · Kommerzielle und strategische Vertragsabwicklung: Entwurf und Verhandlung einer breiten Palette von Life-Science-Verträgen, einschließlich MSAs, Lizenzen, Asset-Käufen, Herstellungsverträgen und Vereinbarungen über klinische Studien (CTAs). · Führung im Datenschutz: Überwachen Sie die Einhaltung des Schweizer DSG und der EU-DSGVO und bieten Sie fachkundige Beratung zum Datenschutz bei klinischen Studien, Patientendaten und grenzüberschreitenden Übermittlungen. · Risikominderung: Identifizieren und antizipieren Sie rechtliche Risiken bei Geschäftsaktivitäten und bieten Sie umsetzbare Lösungen, um Ziele zu erreichen und gleichzeitig die Rechte des Unternehmens zu schützen. · Compliance im Gesundheitswesen: Wenden Sie umfassende Kenntnisse branchenspezifischer Rahmenbedingungen an, einschließlich der Gesetze zur Bekämpfung von Bestechung und Korruption sowie der Interaktion mit medizinischem Fachpersonal. · Interne Schulung und Weiterentwicklung: Steigern Sie das Rechtsbewusstsein im gesamten Unternehmen, indem Sie Mitarbeiter schulen und interne Richtlinien und Vorlagen aktualisieren. · Streitbeilegung: Verwalten Sie vorgerichtliche Strategien und lösen Sie Streitigkeiten pragmatisch, indem Sie bei Bedarf externe Rechtsbeistände hinzuziehen.
descriptionByLocale.fr:
Debiopharm est une société biopharmaceutique indépendante basée en Suisse qui s'engage en permanence dans la recherche en oncologie et en maladies infectieuses afin d'améliorer les résultats et la qualité de vie des patients. Nos principales activités comprennent le développement de médicaments, la fabrication de médicaments et l’investissement dans la santé numérique. Debiopharm International SA a pour objectif de développer des thérapies innovantes ciblant d'importants besoins médicaux non satisfaits. Notre modèle commercial unique de « développement uniquement » nous permet de servir de pont entre les produits de découverte révolutionnaires et l'accès des patients aux marchés internationaux. Nous identifions d'abord les composés à fort potentiel pour une licence, démontrons cliniquement leur sécurité et leur efficacité, puis sélectionnons de grands partenaires de commercialisation pharmaceutique pour maximiser l'accès des patients à l'échelle mondiale. Chez Debiopharm, vous serez à la pointe de la lutte contre le cancer et les maladies infectieuses, et vous aurez l'opportunité de travailler sur de nouveaux médicaments innovants pour les patients. L’efficacité est notre mode d’action, sauver des vies notre objectif collectif. Nous recherchons actuellement un professionnel motivé et collaboratif pour rejoindre notre service juridique basé à notre siège social à Lausanne, en Suisse, en tant que conseiller juridique principal – Commercial et confidentialité Rôle permanent | Lausanne, Suisse 
• Votre mission : Protéger l'innovation grâce à l'excellence juridique En tant que conseiller juridique – Commercial et confidentialité, vous servirez de partenaire pragmatique et orienté business pour nos équipes commerciales, cliniques et opérationnelles. Vous sauvegarderez et développerez les intérêts de Debiopharm en gérant un portefeuille diversifié de contrats complexes et en pilotant notre programme mondial de conformité en matière de protection des données. Vos principales responsabilités · Contrats commerciaux et stratégiques : Rédiger et négocier un large éventail d'accords dans le domaine des sciences de la vie, notamment des MSA, des licences, des achats d'actifs, des accords de fabrication et d'essais cliniques (CTA). · Leadership en matière de confidentialité des données : superviser la conformité avec le LPD suisse et le RGPD de l'UE, en fournissant des conseils d'experts sur la confidentialité des essais cliniques, les données des patients et les transferts transfrontaliers. · Atténuation des risques : identifier et anticiper les risques juridiques dans les activités commerciales, en proposant des solutions concrètes pour faciliter les objectifs tout en protégeant les droits de l'entreprise. · Conformité des soins de santé : appliquer une solide connaissance des cadres spécifiques au secteur, y compris les lois anti-corruption et les interactions avec les professionnels de la santé. · Formation et évolution internes : améliorez la sensibilisation juridique dans toute l'organisation en formant les employés et en mettant à jour les politiques et modèles internes. · Gestion des litiges : gérer les stratégies précontentieuses et résoudre les litiges de manière pragmatique, en dirigeant des conseils externes si nécessaire.

jobs.title: P2612 - Senior Manager Statistical Programming
jobs.description:
Together, we innovate, we impact, we grow !

At Debiopharm, every step we take is guided by one purpose: improving the lives of people affected by cancer and infectious diseases. As a privately-owned Swiss biopharmaceutical company, we’re driven by science, but above all, by the people behind every treatment — the patients and their families.

Through our unique “development only” model, we bring forward promising therapies and transform them into treatments that can reach those who need them most, faster.

We are currently looking for a motivated and collaborative professional to join our Biostatistics and Data Management (R&D) Department based at our Headquarters in Lausanne, Switzerland as

Senior Manager, Statistical Programming

Permanent role | Lausanne, Switzerland

Your Mission:

As the Senior Manager of Statistical Programming, you will provide strategic leadership for Statistical and Clinical Data Science Programming. You will be the driving force behind our digitalization strategy, implementing cutting-edge data science technology and AI adoption to streamline clinical data processing and analysis. Beyond strategy, you will lead a talented team of programmers and data scientists to ensure the highest quality of deliverables for our clinical projects.

Your Key 
• Responsibilities

· Leadership & Management: Manage a team of statistical programmers, overseeing resource planning, talent acquisition, and professional development.

· Strategic Digitalization: Lead the roadmap for AI adoption and new technical solutions for integrating and reporting clinical data.

· Operational Excellence: Accountable for the quality and timelines of all programming deliverables (datasets, TLFs) for study reports and integrated summaries.

· Vendor Oversight: Provide technical guidance to CRO partners to ensure efficient, integrated project reporting and adherence to standards.

· Compliance: Ensure all activities strictly adhere to GCP, SOPs, and regulatory requirements for electronic data submissions.

· Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner closely with Biostatistics, Data Management, IT, and Clinical Development functions.

jobs.title: P2613 - CSV Lead
jobs.description:
Together, we innovate, we impact, we grow !

At Debiopharm, every step we take is guided by one purpose: improving the lives of people affected by cancer and infectious diseases. As a privately-owned Swiss biopharmaceutical company, we’re driven by science, but above all, by the people behind every treatment — the patients and their families.

Through our unique “development only” model, we bring forward promising therapies and transform them into treatments that can reach those who need them most, faster.

We are currently looking for a strategic and hands-on Expert to join our Quality Management team in Lausanne, Switzerland. In this high-impact role, you will drive Computerized System Validation (CSV) activities to support our ambitious digitalization initiatives, ensuring our systems remain compliant, efficient, and ready for the future, as a

CSV Lead

Permanent role | Lausanne, Switzerland

Your Mission:

Your mission is to lead and optimize the CSV strategy for Debiopharm International SA (DPI). You will bridge the gap between innovation and compliance, ensuring that our digital evolution is supported by a pragmatic, risk-based validation framework that protects data integrity and patient safety across our portfolio.

Your Key 
• Responsibilities

· Strategy & Oversight: Evaluate, design, and implement the CSV strategy for both current and new systems, ensuring it is accurately reflected in procedural documents.

· Vendor & Project Integration: Contribute to vendor selection and qualification, providing pro-active quality oversight for GxP critical systems and processes.

· Execution & Validation: Direct the CSV approach for project teams, supervising the creation and approval of deliverables, test defects, and risk mitigation activities.

· Lifecycle Management: Oversee the computerized system portfolio, managing change controls, periodic reviews, and maintenance activities after "Business Go-live".

· Internal Advisory: Act as an expert advisor on CSV regulations, supporting AI projects from a regulatory/validation standpoint and mentoring staff on CSLC activities.

· Leadership & Quality: Manage, mentor and develop the CSV team, ensuring high-quality delivery across all portfolio initiatives, identify areas for process improvement, change management and assist with audit planning / CAPA formulation.

jobs.title: P2615 - Clinical Operational Manager (COM)
jobs.description:
Together, we innovate, we impact, we grow !

At Debiopharm, every step we take is guided by one purpose: improving the lives of people affected by cancer and infectious diseases. As a privately-owned Swiss biopharmaceutical company, we’re driven by science, but above all, by the people behind every treatment — the patients and their families.

Through our unique “development only” model, we bring forward promising therapies and transform them into treatments that can reach those who need them most, faster.

Are you passionate about advancing science and improving lives? Are you willing to go above and beyond? We are currently looking for a dynamic, driven, proactive and dedicated professional to join our Clinical Operations team based at our Headquarters in Lausanne, Switzerland as

Clinical Operational Manager (COM)

Permanent role | Location: Lausanne, Switzerland

Your Mission

As a Clinical Operational Manager (COM), you are a core member of our Clinical Trial Team, driving the cross-functional delivery of clinical trials from planning through to close-out. You will lead delegated operational activities and may even step into the role of Clinical Trial Leader to operationalize study protocols. This role requires international travel up to 10% of the time.

Key 
• Responsibilities

· Trial Management: Coordinate/lead cross-functional teams, organize meetings, and track critical actions to ensure trial progress.

· Vendor Management: Oversee CRO/vendor performance and manage delegated operational activity/plans (e.g project management, communication, risk, and quality).

· Operational Excellence: Ensure trial deliverables meet timelines, budget, and quality standards, while managing trial documentation in the TMF.

· Reporting: Collect and track trial status such as enrolment and timelines to monitor the trial progress and to update the relevant internal/external boards (e.g., Core Team, Development Leadership Team, and Leadership Team).

· Clinical Supply: Coordinate with Clinical Trial Supply Managers to ensure Investigational Medicinal Products (IMP) reach regions and countries effectively.

· Compliance: Contribute to risk and issue identification, develop mitigation and corrective plans, ensure all activities remain compliant with GCP and are inspection-ready.

jobs.description:
Together, we innovate, we impact, we grow !

At Debiopharm, every step we take is guided by one purpose: improving the lives of people affected by cancer and infectious diseases. Clinical Operational Specialist (COS)

Contractor role | Location: Lausanne, Switzerland

Your Mission

The Clinical Operational Specialist (COS) is a vital member of the Clinical Trial Team, supporting the seamless delivery of clinical trials under the leadership of the Clinical Trial Leader. You will be responsible for delegated operational activities, ensuring high quality and precision from trial setup through to archiving.

Key 
• Responsibilities

· Trial Coordination: Manage Clinical Trial Team meetings, prepare minutes, and follow up on outstanding actions.

· Trial management: Ensure all delegated operational trial deliverables are met according to timelines, budget, operational procedures, and quality standards.

· Reporting: Collect and track trial status such as enrolment and timelines to monitor the trial progress and to update the relevant internal/external boards (e.g., Core Team, Development Leadership Team, and Leadership Team).

· TMF Management: Collaborate with TMF Specialists to set up, maintain, and archive the Trial Master File, ensuring constant audit readiness.

· Operational Setup: Manage & oversee IRB/IEC submission plans, set up clinical trial insurance, and oversee vendor systems set up/management such as IRT.

· Vendor Oversight: Assist with CRO/vendor oversight/tracking, invoice reconciliation, and purchasing order generation.

· Communication: Prepare materials for investigator meetings, advisory boards, and internal newsletters.

jobs.title: T2604 - Research Fellow - AI-Enhanced Pharmacometrics (Intern)
jobs.description:
At Debiopharm, every step we take is guided by one purpose: improving the lives of people affected by cancer and infectious diseases. As a privately-owned Swiss biopharmaceutical company, we’re driven by science, but above all, by the people behind every treatment — the patients and their families.

Through our unique “development only” model, we bring forward promising therapies and transform them into treatments that can reach those who need them most, faster.

Are you ready to be at the forefront of the model-informed drug development (MIDD)? Debiopharm International SA is seeking a highly motivated Research Fellow (Intern) to help build an AI-enhanced Quantitative Systems Pharmacology (QSP) platform. This project offers a unique opportunity to integrate preclinical, clinical data and real-world data with mathematical models and AI to accelerate the development of our Antibody-Drug Conjugate (ADC) pipeline. You will work directly on assets, bridging the gap between systems biology and data-driven decision-making.

Research Fellow – AI-Enhanced Pharmacometrics (Intern)

Location: Lausanne, Switzerland

Department: Clinical Pharmacology & Pharmacometrics

Project: 12-months internship

Executive Summary of the Internship

The primary mission of this fellowship is to develop and standardize a custom QSP platform for the Debiopharm ADC pipeline. This role is central to a specific project strategy, aiming to shorten development timelines and enhance our proprietary MIDD capabilities.

Key Responsibilities

- Educational Background: PhD or Master’s student in Pharmacometrics, Biostatistics, Computational Biology, Mathematics, Computer Science, or Engineering.

- Team Synergy: Ability to work independently on technical tasks while collaborating effectively in a team environment.
- Organization: Strong organizational skills to manage and process data from multiple ADC programs simultaneously.
- Communication: Fluency in English (both oral and written).

## Benefits
- Being part of a company where innovation, collaboration, and impact aren’t just values — they’re how we work every day
- Partner with teams across disciplines, at the forefront of oncology and anti-infective development
- An inclusive and respectful workplace — proud to be Equal-Pay certified

- A chance to grow, share, and shape the future of healthcare

descriptionByLocale.fr:
Chez Debiopharm, chaque étape que nous entreprenons est guidée par un seul objectif : améliorer la vie des personnes touchées par le cancer et les maladies infectieuses. En tant qu’entreprise biopharmaceutique suisse privée, nous sommes motivés par la science, mais surtout par les personnes derrière chaque traitement : les patients et leurs familles. Grâce à notre modèle unique de « développement uniquement », nous proposons des thérapies prometteuses et les transformons en traitements qui peuvent atteindre plus rapidement ceux qui en ont le plus besoin. Debiopharm International SA est à la recherche d'un chercheur (stagiaire) très motivé pour aider à créer une plateforme de pharmacologie quantitative des systèmes (QSP) améliorée par l'IA. Ce projet offre une opportunité unique d'intégrer des données précliniques, cliniques et des données du monde réel avec des modèles mathématiques et l'IA pour accélérer le développement de notre pipeline de conjugués anticorps-médicament (ADC). Vous travaillerez directement sur les actifs, comblant ainsi le fossé entre la biologie des systèmes et la prise de décision basée sur les données. Chercheur – AI-Enhanced Pharmacometrics (Stagiaire) Lieu : Lausanne, Suisse Département : Pharmacologie clinique et pharmacométrie Projet : stage de 12 mois Résumé du stage La mission principale de cette bourse est de développer et de standardiser une plateforme QSP personnalisée pour le pipeline Debiopharm ADC. En utilisant l'IA et des techniques de modélisation avancées, vous contribuerez à un environnement « Digital Twin » pour simuler des essais virtuels, optimiser les schémas posologiques et établir des critères « Go/No-Go ». Ce rôle est au cœur d'une stratégie de projet spécifique, visant à raccourcir les délais de développement et à améliorer nos capacités exclusives MIDD. Responsabilités clés ## 
• Exigences - Formation : Étudiant au doctorat ou à la maîtrise en pharmacométrie, biostatistique, biologie computationnelle, mathématiques, informatique ou ingénierie. - Connaissances de base : Comprendre les concepts de base de la pharmacocinétique (PK) et de la pharmacodynamique (PD). - Logiciel de modélisation : Une solide maîtrise de R ou Python est essentielle. Une exposition préalable à Monolix, NONMEM, Matlab ou SimBiology est un avantage significatif. La maîtrise du C++ est considérée comme un atout majeur pour le développement de simulateurs. - Synergie d'équipe : Capacité à travailler de manière indépendante sur des tâches techniques tout en collaborant efficacement dans un environnement d'équipe. - Organisation : Solides compétences organisationnelles pour gérer et traiter simultanément les données de plusieurs programmes ADC. - Communication : Maîtrise de l'anglais (à l'oral et à l'écrit). ## Avantages - Faire partie d'une entreprise où l'innovation, la collaboration et l'impact ne sont pas seulement des valeurs, c'est notre façon de travailler au quotidien - Collaborer avec des équipes multidisciplinaires, à la pointe du développement de l'oncologie et des anti-infectieux - Un lieu de travail inclusif et respectueux - fier d'être certifié Equal-Pay - Grandir dans une culture qui valorise les personnes, les objectifs et la performance - Une chance de grandir, de partager et de façonner l'avenir des soins de santé

jobs.description:
Together, we innovate, we impact, we grow

At Debiopharm, every step we take is guided by one purpose: improving the lives of people affected by cancer and infectious diseases. Are you passionate about advancing science and improving lives? We are currently looking for a driven, passionate and collaborative professional to join our Biostatistics and Data Management department based at our Headquarters in Lausanne, Switzerland as

Senior Clinical Data Manager

Permanent role | Location: Lausanne, Switzerland

Your Mission

As a Senior Clinical Data Manager, you will be accountable for the quality and timelines of data management activities across assigned clinical studies. You will play a pivotal role in ensuring that complete, accurate, and consistent clinical data is available to support our internal decision-making processes. Whether managing internal tasks or overseeing activities outsourced to external providers, you are the guardian of data integrity and quality.

Key Responsibilities

## Requirements
- Education: Scientific or technical background, ideally at a university level. In addition, any Project Management certification would be a plus.
- Experience: At least 10 years in clinical data management, working in matrix organization with at least one previous and solid experience in pharmaceutical industry and supporting Phase 1 to 3 studies. Project Management experience, an asset.
- Technical Expertise: Strong experience with various EDC systems and   first experience or proficiency automating activities with AI.
- Leadership: Proven track record in leading data management studies and teams to deliver high-quality, regulatory-compliant databases.
- Skills: Excellent organizational, planning/coordination, proactivity and communication skills. Tech savvy, especially with Digital tools and AI
- Languages: Fluent in English; knowledge of French is a distinct asset.

- Being part of a company where innovation, collaboration, and impact aren’t just values — they’re how we work every day

jobs.title: T2606 - IT Project Manager (12 months assignment)
jobs.description:
Together, we innovate, we impact, we grow

At Debiopharm, we develop innovative therapies that change patients' lives — and our Digital & IT team is the engine that keeps our science moving. We're looking for an experienced IT Project Manager to join us on a contract basis and drive the delivery of projects that translate our Digital & IT roadmap into business value across R&D, Clinical, Safety, Regulatory, and Quality.

If you thrive at the intersection of technology, business, and regulated environments — we would like to get in touch.

IT Project Manager (Contractor 12 Months)

Lausanne, Switzerland · Hybrid · 12 Months · Contractor role

What you'll do

## Requirements
- 5+ years managing IT projects, ideally in pharma, life sciences, or another regulated industry
- University degree in IT, Engineering, or equivalent
- Working knowledge of CSV / GAMP 5 and IT Systems Lifecycle
- Hands-on experience with both waterfall and agile/hybrid delivery
- Proficiency with MS Planner Premium (or similar) and Azure DevOps (or equivalent), Planisware Orchestra is a plus
- Strong business analysis skills — requirements gathering, process mapping, solution design
- Excellent written and spoken English; French is a strong asset
- Exposure to pharmaceutical R&D, Clinical, Regulatory, or Quality domains is a plus
- Autonomous, structured, resilient — you keep teams steady through every phase
- A strong communicator who can move fluidly from technical detail to executive summary
- Problem solver helping teams navigate unclarity and push through challenges with clear next steps
- Curious, service-minded, and detail-oriented — comfortable challenging stakeholders constructively
- Cool under pressure, with an eye on the bigger picture even when things get busy

## Benefits
- Being part of a company where innovation, collaboration, and impact aren’t just values — they’re how we work every day
- Partner with teams across disciplines, at the forefront of oncology and anti-infective development
- An inclusive and respectful workplace — proud to be Equal-Pay certified
- Grow in a culture that values people, purpose, and performance
- A chance to grow, share, and shape the future of healthcare

jobs.title: P2608 - Head of Biostatistics & Data Management
jobs.description:
At Debiopharm, every step we take is guided by one purpose: improving the lives of people affected by cancer and infectious diseases. As a privately-owned Swiss biopharmaceutical company, we’re driven by science, but above all, by the people behind every treatment — the patients and their families.

Through our unique “development only” model, we bring forward promising therapies and transform them into treatments that can reach those who need them most, faster.

Are you passionate about advancing science and improving lives? We are currently looking for a driven, passionate and strategic leader to join our Biostatistics and Data Management department based at our Headquarters in Lausanne, Switzerland as

Head of Biostatistics and Data Management

At Debiopharm, we are driven by a singular mission: to develop for patients. As the Head of Biostatistics & Data Management, you will play a pivotal role in this mission by leading a dedicated team of highly skilled professionals to provide cutting-edge expertise to all our clinical programs. In this pivotal role, you will design and execute the innovative data strategy that underpins our clinical development pipeline, ensuring the highest standards of scientific rigor and regulatory compliance across all trials and study phases, fundamental to our global product registration efforts.

Your Mission

More than a checklist of skills, we’re looking for someone who shares our commitment to science with purpose.

descriptionByLocale.it: In Debiopharm, ogni passo che facciamo è guidato da un unico scopo: migliorare la vita delle persone colpite da cancro e malattie infettive. In quanto azienda biofarmaceutica svizzera di proprietà privata, siamo guidati dalla scienza, ma soprattutto dalle persone dietro ogni trattamento: i pazienti e le loro famiglie. Attraverso il nostro esclusivo modello di “solo sviluppo”, portiamo avanti terapie promettenti e le trasformiamo in trattamenti che possono raggiungere coloro che ne hanno più bisogno, più velocemente. Ti appassiona il progresso della scienza e il miglioramento della vita? Stiamo attualmente cercando un leader motivato, appassionato e strategico che si unisca al nostro dipartimento di biostatistica e gestione dei dati con sede presso la nostra sede centrale a Losanna, in Svizzera, come responsabile della biostatistica e della gestione dei dati. In Debiopharm, siamo guidati da una missione singolare: sviluppare per i pazienti. In qualità di responsabile della biostatistica e della gestione dei dati, svolgerai un ruolo fondamentale in questa missione guidando un team dedicato di professionisti altamente qualificati per fornire competenze all'avanguardia a tutti i nostri programmi clinici. In questo ruolo fondamentale, progetterai ed eseguirai l'innovativa strategia dei dati che è alla base della nostra pipeline di sviluppo clinico, garantendo i più elevati standard di rigore scientifico e conformità normativa in tutte le sperimentazioni e le fasi di studio, fondamentali per i nostri sforzi globali di registrazione dei prodotti. La tua missione Più che un elenco di competenze, stiamo cercando qualcuno che condivida il nostro impegno per la scienza con uno scopo.
descriptionByLocale.de: Bei Debiopharm ist jeder Schritt, den wir unternehmen, von einem Ziel geleitet: der Verbesserung des Lebens von Menschen, die von Krebs und Infektionskrankheiten betroffen sind. Als privat geführtes Schweizer biopharmazeutisches Unternehmen sind wir von der Wissenschaft angetrieben, vor allem aber von den Menschen, die hinter jeder Behandlung stehen – den Patienten und ihren Familien. Durch unser einzigartiges „Development Only“-Modell bringen wir vielversprechende Therapien voran und wandeln sie in Behandlungen um, die schneller diejenigen erreichen können, die sie am meisten benötigen. Wir sind derzeit auf der Suche nach einer engagierten, leidenschaftlichen und strategischen Führungskraft für die Verstärkung unserer Abteilung für Biostatistik und Datenmanagement an unserem Hauptsitz in Lausanne, Schweiz, als Leiterin für Biostatistik und Datenmanagement. Bei Debiopharm werden wir von einer einzigen Mission angetrieben: für Patienten zu entwickeln. Als Leiter der Abteilung Biostatistik und Datenmanagement spielen Sie eine entscheidende Rolle bei dieser Mission, indem Sie ein engagiertes Team hochqualifizierter Fachleute leiten, um allen unseren klinischen Programmen modernstes Fachwissen zur Verfügung zu stellen. In dieser Schlüsselrolle entwerfen und implementieren Sie die innovative Datenstrategie, die unserer klinischen Entwicklungspipeline zugrunde liegt, und stellen die höchsten Standards der wissenschaftlichen Genauigkeit und Einhaltung gesetzlicher Vorschriften in allen Versuchen und Studienphasen sicher, die für unsere globalen Produktregistrierungsbemühungen von grundlegender Bedeutung sind. jobs.title: P2606 - Global Early Commercial Manager, Oncology
jobs.description:
At Debiopharm, every step we take is guided by one purpose: improving the lives of people affected by cancer and infectious diseases. As a privately-owned Swiss biopharmaceutical company, we’re driven by science, but above all, by the people behind every treatment — the patients and their families.

Through our unique “development only” model, we bring forward promising therapies and transform them into treatments that can reach those who need them most, faster.

Are you ready to bridge the gap between breakthrough science and commercial success?

We are currently looking for a dynamic Global Early Commercial Manager to shape the future of our oncology portfolio. In this pivotal role, you will be the commercial voice within Core Project Teams, driving the strategy for assets from preclinical stages through the start of Phase 3. Your mission is to maximize asset value and ensure our innovations meet the real-world needs of patients and providers. The role will be based at our Headquarters in Lausanne, Switzerland.

Global Early Commercial Manager, Oncology

Location: Lausanne, Switzerland

Department: Business and Program Leadership & Management; Commercial Strategy

Contract: permanent role, full-time.

Executive Summary of Responsibilities

As the Global Early Commercial Manager, you are responsible for building and integrating marketing and commercial strategies into Debiopharm’s portfolio development plans. You will act as a strategic champion, transforming market insights into actionable product positioning, refining Target Product Profiles (TPP), and collaborating cross-functionally to ensure our oncology pipeline is competitively differentiated and ready for successful out-licensing or launch.

## Requirements
- Market Insight Integration: Gather and analyze global market insights from KOLs, advisory boards, and patient organizations to shape product development strategy.
- Strategic Planning: Lead the creation of market-driven disease area strategies and refine Target Out-licensing Profiles (TOP).
- Landscape Assessment: Perform deep dives into market dynamics, patient journeys, and "white space" to drive product positioning and "war gaming" exercises.
- Clinical Collaboration: Partner with Clinical Operations to optimize trial enrollment and retention through targeted recruitment campaigns and investigator engagement.
- Value Proposition: Optimize the Target Product Profile (TPP) to ensure a clearly differentiated competitive advantage and clinical value.
- Stakeholder Management: Build productive relationships across PMO, Medical, Legal, and Regulatory teams to ensure strategic alignment.
- Advocacy & Awareness: Create advocacy plans with medical teams and collaborate with Communications to build early product awareness.
- Business Development Support: Assist the licensing team in assessing business opportunities for new products using market intelligence.
- Educational Foundation: Bachelor’s degree in science or business, ideally complemented by an MBA or PhD.
- Oncology Marketing Expertise: 5–10 years of robust marketing experience in mid-to-large pharma, specifically featuring in-line brand marketing within the Oncology therapeutic area.
- Strategic Lifecycle Management: Broad commercial and pre-launch experience with a thorough mastery of product positioning, brand development, and identifying critical commercial touchpoints.
- Insight-Driven Strategy: Proven ability to translate complex insights into effective marketing strategies and market-shaping initiatives by leveraging deep knowledge of patients, prescribers, payers, and providers.
- Cross-Functional & Matrix Leadership: Expert at leading cross-functional project teams and motivating agency partners within complex international matrix environments.
- Influential Stakeholder Management: Master of "influence without authority," capable of building trusted relationships across all levels and resolving conflicts through active listening and sound judgment.
- Entrepreneurial Agility: A proactive, decisive professional who thrives in ambiguity, fosters a culture of constructive challenge , and possesses an entrepreneurial mindset for exploring new ways of working.
- R&D & Commercial Integration: Deep understanding of the R&D process with a track record of integrating commercial perspectives into R&D to positively influence development projects.

descriptionByLocale.en:
At Debiopharm, every step we take is guided by one purpose: improving the lives of people affected by cancer and infectious diseases. As a privately-owned Swiss biopharmaceutical company, we’re driven by science, but above all, by the people behind every treatment — the patients and their families.

Through our unique “development only” model, we bring forward promising therapies and transform them into treatments that can reach those who need them most, faster.

Are you ready to bridge the gap between breakthrough science and commercial success?

We are currently looking for a dynamic Global Early Commercial Manager to shape the future of our oncology portfolio. In this pivotal role, you will be the commercial voice within Core Project Teams, driving the strategy for assets from preclinical stages through the start of Phase 3. Your mission is to maximize asset value and ensure our innovations meet the real-world needs of patients and providers. The role will be based at our Headquarters in Lausanne, Switzerland.

Global Early Commercial Manager, Oncology

Location: Lausanne, Switzerland

Department: Business and Program Leadership & Management; Commercial Strategy

Contract: permanent role, full-time.

Executive Summary of Responsibilities

As the Global Early Commercial Manager, you are responsible for building and integrating marketing and commercial strategies into Debiopharm’s portfolio development plans. You will act as a strategic champion, transforming market insights into actionable product positioning, refining Target Product Profiles (TPP), and collaborating cross-functionally to ensure our oncology pipeline is competitively differentiated and ready for successful out-licensing or launch.

## Requirements
- Market Insight Integration: Gather and analyze global market insights from KOLs, advisory boards, and patient organizations to shape product development strategy.
- Strategic Planning: Lead the creation of market-driven disease area strategies and refine Target Out-licensing Profiles (TOP).
- Landscape Assessment: Perform deep dives into market dynamics, patient journeys, and "white space" to drive product positioning and "war gaming" exercises.
- Clinical Collaboration: Partner with Clinical Operations to optimize trial enrollment and retention through targeted recruitment campaigns and investigator engagement.
- Value Proposition: Optimize the Target Product Profile (TPP) to ensure a clearly differentiated competitive advantage and clinical value.
- Stakeholder Management: Build productive relationships across PMO, Medical, Legal, and Regulatory teams to ensure strategic alignment.
- Advocacy & Awareness: Create advocacy plans with medical teams and collaborate with Communications to build early product awareness.
- Business Development Support: Assist the licensing team in assessing business opportunities for new products using market intelligence.
- Educational Foundation: Bachelor’s degree in science or business, ideally complemented by an MBA or PhD.
- Oncology Marketing Expertise: 5–10 years of robust marketing experience in mid-to-large pharma, specifically featuring in-line brand marketing within the Oncology therapeutic area.
- Strategic Lifecycle Management: Broad commercial and pre-launch experience with a thorough mastery of product positioning, brand development, and identifying critical commercial touchpoints.
- Insight-Driven Strategy: Proven ability to translate complex insights into effective marketing strategies and market-shaping initiatives by leveraging deep knowledge of patients, prescribers, payers, and providers.
- Cross-Functional & Matrix Leadership: Expert at leading cross-functional project teams and motivating agency partners within complex international matrix environments.
- Influential Stakeholder Management: Master of "influence without authority," capable of building trusted relationships across all levels and resolving conflicts through active listening and sound judgment.
- Entrepreneurial Agility: A proactive, decisive professional who thrives in ambiguity, fosters a culture of constructive challenge , and possesses an entrepreneurial mindset for exploring new ways of working.
- R&D & Commercial Integration: Deep understanding of the R&D process with a track record of integrating commercial perspectives into R&D to positively influence development projects.
descriptionByLocale.it:
In Debiopharm, ogni passo che facciamo è guidato da un unico scopo: migliorare la vita delle persone colpite da cancro e malattie infettive. In quanto azienda biofarmaceutica svizzera di proprietà privata, siamo guidati dalla scienza, ma soprattutto dalle persone dietro ogni trattamento: i pazienti e le loro famiglie. Attraverso il nostro esclusivo modello di “solo sviluppo”, portiamo avanti terapie promettenti e le trasformiamo in trattamenti che possono raggiungere coloro che ne hanno più bisogno, più velocemente. Sei pronto a colmare il divario tra la scienza rivoluzionaria e il successo commerciale? Stiamo attualmente cercando un dinamico Global Early Commercial Manager per dare forma al futuro del nostro portafoglio oncologico. In questo ruolo fondamentale, sarai la voce commerciale all'interno dei Core Project Teams, guidando la strategia per le risorse dalle fasi precliniche fino all'inizio della Fase 3. La tua missione è massimizzare il valore delle risorse e garantire che le nostre innovazioni soddisfino le esigenze reali di pazienti e fornitori. Il ruolo avrà sede presso la nostra sede centrale a Losanna, Svizzera. Global Early Commercial Manager, Oncologia Sede: Losanna, Svizzera Dipartimento: Leadership e gestione aziendale e di programma; Contratto di strategia commerciale: ruolo a tempo indeterminato, full-time. Riepilogo esecutivo delle responsabilità In qualità di Global Early Commercial Manager, sei responsabile della creazione e dell'integrazione di strategie commerciali e di marketing nei piani di sviluppo del portafoglio di Debiopharm. Agirai come un campione strategico, trasformando le informazioni di mercato in un posizionamento di prodotto attuabile, perfezionando i profili di prodotto target (TPP) e collaborando in modo interfunzionale per garantire che la nostra pipeline oncologica sia differenziata in modo competitivo e pronta per la concessione in licenza o il lancio di successo. ## 
• Requisiti - Integrazione delle informazioni di mercato: raccogli e analizza le informazioni sul mercato globale provenienti da KOL, comitati consultivi e organizzazioni di pazienti per definire la strategia di sviluppo del prodotto. - Pianificazione strategica: guidare la creazione di strategie per aree di malattia guidate dal mercato e perfezionare i profili di out-licensing target (TOP). - Valutazione del panorama: eseguire approfondimenti sulle dinamiche di mercato, sui percorsi dei pazienti e sugli "spazi bianchi" per guidare il posizionamento del prodotto e gli esercizi di "gioco di guerra". - Collaborazione clinica: collaborare con Clinical Operations per ottimizzare l'arruolamento e la fidelizzazione degli studi attraverso campagne di reclutamento mirate e il coinvolgimento degli investigatori. - Proposta di valore: ottimizzare il profilo del prodotto target (TPP) per garantire un vantaggio competitivo e un valore clinico chiaramente differenziati. - Gestione delle parti interessate: costruire relazioni produttive tra i team PMO, medici, legali e normativi per garantire l'allineamento strategico. - Patrocinio e sensibilizzazione: crea piani di patrocinio con i team medici e collabora con le comunicazioni per creare una precoce consapevolezza del prodotto. - Supporto allo sviluppo aziendale: assistere il team addetto alle licenze nella valutazione delle opportunità commerciali per nuovi prodotti utilizzando l'intelligence di mercato. - Base educativa: laurea in scienze o economia, idealmente integrata da un MBA o un dottorato di ricerca. - Competenza nel marketing oncologico: 5-10 anni di solida esperienza di marketing nel settore farmaceutico di medie e grandi dimensioni, in particolare con marketing del marchio in linea all'interno dell'area terapeutica oncologica. - Gestione strategica del ciclo di vita: ampia esperienza commerciale e pre-lancio con una padronanza approfondita del posizionamento del prodotto, dello sviluppo del marchio e dell'identificazione dei punti di contatto commerciali critici. - Strategia basata sugli insight: comprovata capacità di tradurre approfondimenti complessi in strategie di marketing efficaci e iniziative di modellamento del mercato sfruttando una conoscenza approfondita di pazienti, prescrittori, contribuenti e fornitori. - Leadership interfunzionale e a matrice: esperto nella guida di team di progetto interfunzionali e nella motivazione di partner di agenzie all'interno di ambienti complessi a matrice internazionale. - Gestione degli stakeholder influenti: Maestro di "influenza senza autorità", capace di costruire relazioni di fiducia a tutti i livelli e risolvere i conflitti attraverso l'ascolto attivo e il buon giudizio. - Agilità imprenditoriale: un professionista proattivo e deciso che prospera nell'ambiguità, promuove una cultura della sfida costruttiva e possiede una mentalità imprenditoriale per esplorare nuovi modi di lavorare. - Ricerca e sviluppo e integrazione commerciale: profonda conoscenza del processo di ricerca e sviluppo con esperienza nell'integrazione delle prospettive commerciali nella ricerca e sviluppo per influenzare positivamente i progetti di sviluppo.
descriptionByLocale.de:
Bei Debiopharm ist jeder Schritt, den wir unternehmen, von einem Ziel geleitet: der Verbesserung des Lebens von Menschen, die von Krebs und Infektionskrankheiten betroffen sind. Als privat geführtes Schweizer biopharmazeutisches Unternehmen sind wir von der Wissenschaft angetrieben, vor allem aber von den Menschen, die hinter jeder Behandlung stehen – den Patienten und ihren Familien. Durch unser einzigartiges „Development Only“-Modell bringen wir vielversprechende Therapien voran und wandeln sie in Behandlungen um, die schneller diejenigen erreichen können, die sie am meisten benötigen. Sind Sie bereit, die Lücke zwischen bahnbrechender Wissenschaft und kommerziellem Erfolg zu schließen? Wir sind derzeit auf der Suche nach einem dynamischen Global Early Commercial Manager, der die Zukunft unseres Onkologie-Portfolios gestalten soll. In dieser Schlüsselrolle sind Sie die kommerzielle Stimme innerhalb der Kernprojektteams und treiben die Strategie für Vermögenswerte von der präklinischen Phase bis zum Beginn von Phase 3 voran. Ihre Mission ist es, den Wert der Vermögenswerte zu maximieren und sicherzustellen, dass unsere Innovationen den realen Bedürfnissen von Patienten und Anbietern entsprechen. Die Stelle wird an unserem Hauptsitz in Lausanne, Schweiz, angesiedelt sein. Global Early Commercial Manager, Onkologie Standort: Lausanne, Schweiz Abteilung: Geschäfts- und Programmführung und -management; Kommerzieller Strategievertrag: Festanstellung, Vollzeit. Zusammenfassung der Verantwortlichkeiten Als Global Early Commercial Manager sind Sie für den Aufbau und die Integration von Marketing- und kommerziellen Strategien in die Portfolio-Entwicklungspläne von Debiopharm verantwortlich. Sie fungieren als strategischer Champion, wandeln Markteinblicke in umsetzbare Produktpositionierung um, verfeinern Zielproduktprofile (TPP) und arbeiten funktionsübergreifend zusammen, um sicherzustellen, dass unsere Onkologie-Pipeline wettbewerbsfähig ist und für eine erfolgreiche Auslizenzierung oder Markteinführung bereit ist. ## 
• Anforderungen – Integration von Markteinblicken: Sammeln und analysieren Sie globale Markteinblicke von KOLs, Beiräten und Patientenorganisationen, um eine Produktentwicklungsstrategie zu gestalten. - Strategische Planung: Leiten Sie die Entwicklung marktorientierter Strategien für Krankheitsbereiche und verfeinern Sie Target Out-Licensing Profiles (TOP). - Landschaftsbewertung: Führen Sie tiefe Einblicke in die Marktdynamik, Patientenreisen und „Leerräume“ durch, um die Produktpositionierung und „War Gaming“-Übungen voranzutreiben. - Klinische Zusammenarbeit: Arbeiten Sie mit Clinical Operations zusammen, um die Registrierung und Bindung an Studien durch gezielte Rekrutierungskampagnen und die Einbindung von Forschern zu optimieren. - Wertversprechen: Optimieren Sie das Zielproduktprofil (TPP), um einen klar differenzierten Wettbewerbsvorteil und klinischen Wert sicherzustellen. - Stakeholder-Management: Bauen Sie produktive Beziehungen zwischen PMO-, Medizin-, Rechts- und Regulierungsteams auf, um die strategische Ausrichtung sicherzustellen. - Advocacy & Awareness: Erstellen Sie Advocacy-Pläne mit medizinischen Teams und arbeiten Sie mit der Kommunikation zusammen, um frühzeitig Produktbewusstsein aufzubauen. - Unterstützung der Geschäftsentwicklung: Unterstützen Sie das Lizenzierungsteam bei der Bewertung von Geschäftsmöglichkeiten für neue Produkte mithilfe von Marktinformationen. - Bildungsgrundlage: Bachelor-Abschluss in Naturwissenschaften oder Wirtschaft, idealerweise ergänzt durch einen MBA oder PhD. - Onkologie-Marketing-Expertise: 5–10 Jahre fundierte Marketingerfahrung im mittleren bis großen Pharmabereich, insbesondere mit Inline-Markenmarketing im Onkologie-Therapiebereich. - Strategisches Lebenszyklusmanagement: Umfangreiche kommerzielle Erfahrung und Erfahrung vor der Markteinführung mit gründlicher Beherrschung der Produktpositionierung, Markenentwicklung und Identifizierung kritischer kommerzieller Berührungspunkte. - Erkenntnisgesteuerte Strategie: Nachgewiesene Fähigkeit, komplexe Erkenntnisse in effektive Marketingstrategien und marktgestaltende Initiativen umzusetzen, indem umfassende Kenntnisse über Patienten, verschreibende Ärzte, Kostenträger und Anbieter genutzt werden. - Cross-Functional & Matrix Leadership: Experte für die Leitung funktionsübergreifender Projektteams und die Motivation von Agenturpartnern in komplexen internationalen Matrixumgebungen. - Einflussreiches Stakeholder-Management: Meister des „Einflusses ohne Autorität“, fähig, vertrauensvolle Beziehungen auf allen Ebenen aufzubauen und Konflikte durch aktives Zuhören und fundiertes Urteilsvermögen zu lösen. - Unternehmerische Agilität: Ein proaktiver, entscheidungsfreudiger Fachmann, der in Unklarheiten aufblüht, eine Kultur der konstruktiven Herausforderung fördert und eine unternehmerische Denkweise für die Erkundung neuer Arbeitsweisen besitzt. - F&E und kommerzielle Integration: Tiefes Verständnis des F&E-Prozesses mit einer Erfolgsbilanz bei der Integration kommerzieller Perspektiven in F&E, um Entwicklungsprojekte positiv zu beeinflussen.
descriptionByLocale.fr:
Chez Debiopharm, chaque étape que nous entreprenons est guidée par un seul objectif : améliorer la vie des personnes touchées par le cancer et les maladies infectieuses. En tant qu’entreprise biopharmaceutique suisse privée, nous sommes motivés par la science, mais surtout par les personnes derrière chaque traitement : les patients et leurs familles. Grâce à notre modèle unique de « développement uniquement », nous proposons des thérapies prometteuses et les transformons en traitements qui peuvent atteindre plus rapidement ceux qui en ont le plus besoin. Êtes-vous prêt à combler le fossé entre les avancées scientifiques et le succès commercial ? Nous recherchons actuellement un Global Early Commercial Manager dynamique pour façonner l’avenir de notre portefeuille d’oncologie. Dans ce rôle clé, vous serez la voix commerciale au sein des équipes de projet principales, dirigeant la stratégie pour les actifs depuis les étapes précliniques jusqu'au début de la phase 3. 
• Votre mission est de maximiser la valeur des actifs et de garantir que nos innovations répondent aux besoins réels des patients et des prestataires. Le poste sera basé à notre siège social à Lausanne, en Suisse. Responsable commercial mondial précoce, oncologie Lieu : Lausanne, Suisse Département : Leadership et gestion des affaires et des programmes ; Contrat de stratégie commerciale : poste permanent, temps plein. Résumé exécutif des responsabilités En tant que Global Early Commercial Manager, vous êtes responsable de l'élaboration et de l'intégration des stratégies marketing et commerciales dans les plans de développement du portefeuille de Debiopharm. Vous agirez en tant que champion stratégique, transformant les informations sur le marché en un positionnement de produit exploitable, affinant les profils de produits cibles (TPP) et collaborant de manière interfonctionnelle pour garantir que notre pipeline d'oncologie est différencié de manière compétitive et prêt pour une attribution de licence ou un lancement réussi. ## 
• Exigences - Intégration des informations sur le marché : rassemblez et analysez les informations sur le marché mondial auprès des KOL, des conseils consultatifs et des organisations de patients pour façonner la stratégie de développement de produits. - Planification stratégique : diriger la création de stratégies de domaines de maladies axées sur le marché et affiner les profils d'octroi de licences cibles (TOP). - Évaluation du paysage : effectuez des analyses approfondies de la dynamique du marché, des parcours des patients et des "espaces blancs" pour piloter le positionnement des produits et les exercices de "jeu de guerre". - Collaboration clinique : collaborez avec les opérations cliniques pour optimiser le recrutement et la rétention des essais grâce à des campagnes de recrutement ciblées et à l'engagement des enquêteurs. - Proposition de valeur : optimiser le profil de produit cible (TPP) pour garantir un avantage concurrentiel et une valeur clinique clairement différenciés. - Gestion des parties prenantes : établir des relations productives entre les équipes PMO, médicales, juridiques et réglementaires pour garantir l'alignement stratégique. - Plaidoyer et sensibilisation : créer des plans de plaidoyer auprès des équipes médicales et collaborer avec les communications pour renforcer la sensibilisation précoce au produit. - Soutien au développement commercial : aider l'équipe des licences à évaluer les opportunités commerciales pour les nouveaux produits en utilisant l'intelligence du marché. - Fondation éducative : baccalauréat en sciences ou en commerce, idéalement complété par un MBA ou un doctorat. - Expertise en marketing en oncologie : 5 à 10 années d'expérience en marketing solide dans le secteur pharmaceutique de taille moyenne à grande, portant spécifiquement sur le marketing de marque en ligne dans le domaine thérapeutique de l'oncologie. - Gestion stratégique du cycle de vie : vaste expérience commerciale et préalable au lancement avec une maîtrise approfondie du positionnement des produits, du développement de la marque et de l'identification des points de contact commerciaux critiques. - Stratégie basée sur la connaissance : capacité avérée à traduire des informations complexes en stratégies de marketing efficaces et en initiatives d'évolution du marché en tirant parti de la connaissance approfondie des patients, des prescripteurs, des payeurs et des prestataires. - Leadership interfonctionnel et matriciel : expert dans la direction d'équipes de projets interfonctionnelles et la motivation des partenaires d'agence dans des environnements matriciels internationaux complexes. - Gestion des parties prenantes influentes : maîtrise de "l'influence sans autorité", capable d'établir des relations de confiance à tous les niveaux et de résoudre les conflits grâce à une écoute active et un bon jugement. - Agilité entrepreneuriale : un professionnel proactif et décisif qui s'épanouit dans l'ambiguïté, favorise une culture de défi constructif et possède un état d'esprit entrepreneurial pour explorer de nouvelles façons de travailler. - R&D et intégration commerciale : compréhension approfondie du processus de R&D avec une expérience dans l'intégration de perspectives commerciales dans la R&D pour influencer positivement les projets de développement.

jobs.title: P2607 - Medical Director, Oncology
jobs.description:
At Debiopharm, every step we take is guided by one purpose: improving the lives of people affected by cancer and infectious diseases. As a privately-owned Swiss biopharmaceutical company, we’re driven by science, but above all, by the people behind every treatment — the patients and their families.

Through our unique “development only” model, we bring forward promising therapies and transform them into treatments that can reach those who need them most, faster.

Are you ready to lead the charge in developing next-generation cancer therapies? Debiopharm International SA is looking for a visionary Medical Director, Oncology to serve as the clinical architect for our global oncology programs. In this high-impact role, you will lead the clinical strategy and end-to-end development of innovative compounds, bridging the gap between early discovery and transformative patient care. If you are a physician-leader driven by scientific curiosity and the desire to redefine the treatment landscape for solid tumors, we want to hear from you.

Medical Director, Oncology

Location: Lausanne, Switzerland

Department: Clinical Development

Contract: permanent role, full-time.

Executive Summary

As a Medical Director, Oncology, you will serve as the visionary clinical leader for the global development of transformative compounds. You will design and execute end-to-end clinical strategies—from First-in-Human through Phase 2—to address critical unmet medical needs and achieve Debiopharm’s overarching business objectives.

Key 
• Responsibilities

· Strategic Leadership: Develop and lead the global clinical strategy for assigned oncology programs, accounting for medical practice trends and the competitive landscape.

· Clinical Deliverables: Provide medical input for all key documents, including study protocols, Investigator Brochures, and regulatory registration dossiers.

· Program Execution: Deliver the Clinical Development Plan (CDP) in collaboration with study teams, ensuring efficient protocol execution and medical monitoring.

· Matrix Collaboration: Drive clinical programs in partnership with Project Management, Clinical Operations, Biostatistics, and Regulatory Affairs to meet decision milestones.

· Decision Support: Generate scenarios and risk-management strategies to inform senior leadership and support decision-making.

· Network Building: Develop and maintain a global network of Key Opinion Leaders (KOLs), investigators, and patient advocacy groups.

· Stakeholder Interaction: Represent the clinical program before regulatory authorities, data monitoring boards, and internal decision boards.

· Business Support: Provide medical expertise to support in-/out-licensing activities and market access strategies.
